title: Bank of America and Goldman Sachs Reset Marvell Stock Price Targets
text: Following Marvell Technology's strong Q4 FY2026 earnings report and robust guidance raise, major analysts Bank of America and Goldman Sachs reset their stock price targets on the company. RBC Capital analyst Srini Pajjuri increased his price target to $200 from $170. B. Riley analyst Craig Ellis raised his price target to $205 from $156. Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya raised his price target to $200 from $125. Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Marvell Technology stock based on 22 Buys and four Holds. The company's stock has surged more than 100% during 2026, recording its 12th all-time closing high this year. Wall Street expects Marvell to report earnings per share of $0.79 for Q1 FY27, implying 27.4% year-over-year growth, with revenue estimated to rise by about 27% to $2.40 billion.
